title: Rava Ladoo

subs.name: roasted rice flour (same quantity) for a gluten-free version — the texture will be finer
refined coconut oil for a dairy-free version with a subtle tropical note
desiccated coconut — use 0.33 cup since it is more concentrated; no pre-frying needed

ingredients.name: warm milk (to bind if needed)
visual_checks: add a teaspoon at a time only if mix won't hold
ingredients.notes: add a teaspoon at a time only if mix won't hold

steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp of the ghee in a kadai over medium flame. Add the broken cashews and stir until they turn pale gold, about 1 minute. Add the raisins and toss for 30 seconds until they puff. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.

Add the remaining ghee to the kadai and tip in the rava. Roast over medium-low heat, stirring continuously, for 7–9 minutes until the rava turns a warm golden colour, smells like warm toast and feels slightly lighter in texture. The grains should flow freely and not clump.

Add the grated coconut and stir for 2–3 more minutes until it dries out slightly and releases a sweet coconut fragrance. Remove the kadai from heat and spread the mixture onto a wide plate to cool for 8–10 minutes until it is just warm, not hot.

Tip in the powdered sugar, cardamom, fried cashews and raisins. Mix thoroughly — the residual warmth helps the sugar bind slightly. Taste and adjust sweetness. The mixture should hold a shape when pressed but crumble apart easily if you do not press it.

Take about 2 heaped tablespoons of mixture and press firmly in your palm, then roll between both palms into a tight, round ladoo. If the mix doesn't hold together, add warm milk one teaspoon at a time and mix again. Shape all the ladoos and arrange on a tray. Leave to firm up for 15–20 minutes before storing in an airtight container.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.

substitution_notes: rava → roasted rice flour (same quantity) for a gluten-free version — the texture will be finer; coconut → desiccated coconut — use 0.33 cup since it is more concentrated; no pre-frying needed; ghee → refined coconut oil for a dairy-free version with a subtle tropical note
